transcript.text: Thank you.
Hi.
Hello.
How are you?
I'm good.
How are you?
Good.
We have, it's funny, was just looking at the screen and we have three note takers from competing companies in this call.
We've been using Fathom, but now we want to see if there's anything like, you know, how, like, you know, what are the other options?
Options.
So we're testing Fireflies and Reed.
So they're all in here together.
That's funny.
I know when I joined, I was like, wow, there's so many people.
And then I'm like, oh, no, there's only note takers.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's just me and my three note takers, my emotional support note takers.
I wonder which one will end up being best.
You have to keep me updated.
Yeah.
Hi, Nicky.
No, think it's like with the note takers, it really is down to like all of them can do like transcript, transcription.

That's like not a big deal, but it's about like whether they can connect the data and like if you can like if they could like integrate with like Slack and like notify me of any action items from like last week from the meeting.
Like that's where I, that's what I want.
Like I don't really, meeting transcription is just like table stakes at this point.
That's basic.
Yeah.
Yeah.
All right.
Cool.
I think Russell are also.
So, he looks like he's on, just on me.
Oh, right.
Yeah, he just got in.
I'm here.
No worries.
Hi, Russell.
Hey there.
All right.
I think we can get started.
So I know Sheeha and Devin can't make it, but we're recording this.
So, before we jump in, I want to just check in and see if there's anything that's top of mind for you.
Nope.
don't think so.
Okay.
Nicky, I know that you've been reviewing the blogs from last week.
think the two that we sent, like, out of the three that we sent over, you edited, like, you provided feedback on two, and then the one that's remaining is the variant alternatives one.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah.
I think that's right.
I'll get that over to you today.
Cool.

I know that you had, like, some feedback around tone, and we've been working to just adjust the writing guidelines and the proofreading checklist to kind of, like, improve the tone and have it be more in line with, like, your expectations.
So I think this batch of content that we sent this week and then the next one should be, like, a lot better in terms of, like, the following, like, the tone requirements.
Cool.
All right.
So I think the big update this week is, like I shared in Slack, I added over 40 new blog topic ideas to the content calendar.
I based the keyword research primarily on Devon's feedback last week, and I used the product feature matrix or, you know, the...
A really big slide that you shared that has like all of the product, like all the product details, we use that to support keyword research.

So I think this new batch of article ideas, especially in the three clusters that Devin highlighted, which are, I believe, contact center operations, agent performance and coaching, and conversational insights and voice of customer.
So the new assignments in these three clusters should be a lot like less basic, like he said.

So they should be like more in line with content around efficiency and just really like targeting like very, very specific pain points rather than going after like the more educational, foundational, top of the funnel content.
So I, once Devin gives me, provides feedback on the new assignments, then we can kind of like refine.
And, you know, I can do, like, another round of keyword research if needed.

And then in the meantime, we did stage the calibration, refresh, Annie, what we, like, what we really just need here is just for you to go in and, like, make sure we didn't screw anything up when we were just, like, updating.
But Webflow is pretty simple, so I doubt that we did.
But once you take a look, you can just push it live and let me know when that's ready.
So that I can update the content to us.
Okay, cool.
I can do that today.
Just take a quick look and QA and then publish it and ping you.
And this is our first piece, right?
Exactly.
That's the first piece.
And then we also want to publish three today or tomorrow.
So we're going to be four.
Let's go.
Yes.
Cool.
And then, yeah, these five are also, we have.
Five new drafts that are ready for your review this week.
So there are some more commercial keywords in there, like best AI agent platforms for contact centers.
These, just kind of as a heads up, these types of blogs, they do tend to get more feedback because we're just talking about Cresta and your competitors for the majority of the article.
So I just want to flag that, you know, in case you like find yourself leaving more comments that it's expected.

So, you know, it's very helpful for us to get tips and insights from you on how we want, how we should position Cresta versus your competitors and any, any like benefits we should be specifically calling out.
um, all right, great.
And then I'm also going to, uh, set up an automation here, uh, so that.
Once a draft is ready for review, Nicky, you're going to get an email.
So it's going to be in your inbox.
I don't know, do you use email like as a company?
Is that something like, are you in your inbox?
Or is it something that you check like once every two days?
No, I'm in my inbox.
Okay.
I'm more in my inbox.
But I'm definitely checking my email.
I'm more in my inbox than I am.
Like that's like guaranteed almost a wrap for me.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah.
Great.
Yeah.
So I'm I'll, I'll set that automation up for you and then we can kind of, um, yeah, if additional people need to be added to it, just let me know.
Okay.
Um, all right.
And then for, sorry.
Oh, sorry.
It was just, no worries.
Um, so for the featured image generation, we discussed that last week.
Um, and Katya, our designer, she was, she did some experiments using.
I the example images that you shared, and the one, like the images that we created with Nano Banana turned out the best.
So let me just like pull this up for you.
So these are just a couple of examples, and these are images that were created for the article, How to Improve Contact Center Productivity.
So the image is kind of a result of the topic of the article.
So I don't know, like, you know, I'm happy to send these over Slack as well, so you can share them internally.
And we can make additional, like tweaks to the prompt so that when we're generating, so like we can change the colors, like the, like any elements.
Like, for example, if you don't want to see headphones or, you know, things like that, we can kind of tailor it based on your expectations.
Okay.
And have these, have these been shared at all with, I know Steve's in our channel.
I don't know if he's had any exposure to any of the creative yet.
I'm, these, I think were ready yesterday.
So I haven't, I'll tag him in Slack and then they can let us know what he thinks.
Okay.

Just to voice it here, I think maybe it's fine for the productivity piece, but I don't want all of our images looking like, um, I don't know, like AI, AI tech, like the classic AI tech look, you know?
Um, I think it's fine here and there, but I think we got to be mindful about that.
And I think it's actually not super in line with like the, the newer brand looking feel.
And so, yeah, I just want to be, be mindful of that.

Yeah, I think any, what we typically do, and this isn't the case with Cresta because you provided us with examples, but what we typically do when a company just doesn't have like a visual brand guideline, we will go to their homepage and then our designer will kind of like come up with a couple of examples just based on the visuals that are on the homepage, the colors, the fonts.
So that's something that we can prep here as well, or if you have like anything new to share, we can also prepare additional examples once Katya is back from PTO.
Aida, did we already give you the whole brand website?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Like we worked on, I think these are like the more like abstract, I have like these gradients.
But, yeah, I'll ping Katya and tell her that we want to do, like, a couple of additional examples, and I'll share them once they're ready.
Thank you.
The idea of, like, leveraging the website as, like, a, as a kind of, like, feedback mechanism, I think is a really good one.
It's almost like if you were, like, sticking it somewhere on the homepage, like, would it still vibe, you know?
Yeah, exactly.
That's good way to think about it.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
I think what I can ask Katya is just be, like, okay, like, ignore everything else.
Just look at the homepage.
Like, what would you do here?
So maybe it's, you know, maybe she comes up with something cool.
And I can actually show you examples from other companies.
So let me just...
So just...
Find it really quickly that are like more abstract, but still like in line with the rest of their site.
So this was for, yeah, so this is for like an AI web development tool.
So it's like super abstract, but like follows like their brand colors, and we can use like different like graphs.
Yeah, this type of stuff works, for sure.
Yeah, yeah.
All right, awesome.
All right, and then for the upcoming holiday planning, so next Thursday is Christmas, and then the week after that, it's January 1st.
So for the next two weeks, my plan was to just provide async updates, but if you want to reschedule the calls, you can also, we can also do that.
So let me know what you prefer.
I know it's.
Like these two weeks are so crazy because everything's getting moved, but I'm happy to reschedule or just do everything async.
I think, especially over the next two weeks, I don't know what everyone's holiday schedule is going to look like.
So I think it probably makes the most sense to do async for the next two weeks.
But I just think we'll have a difficult time finding a slot that works for everyone.
So it probably makes the most sense to go async.
But Russell, let me know if you disagree on that.
No, I think async is totally good.
Okay.
All right.
And then for, so next week, we're going to deliver five articles.
And then the week of December 29th, that's like a quiet week.
And then production is going to resume.
Like when we come back in January, we are, typically we do like these eight weeks, these prints are like eight weeks, but with Crest.
We have 10 weeks precisely to account for this year-end period when lots of people are out of office.
So yeah, you should get five articles next week, and then the first week of January, we're going to resume content production.
Okay, awesome.
I think those are all the updates that I had.
Is there anything else that you wanted to discuss?
Sorry, just to play that back, Aida.
So next week, we have five.
The week after is the quiet week, and then we start again the week after.
Exactly.
cool.
Thank you.
All right.
Okay, in that case, I think we can wrap up early, get you some time back.
And yeah, we'll be on the lookout for the feedback on the new drafts.
And as soon as we publish the...
Three articles from last week.
I will tag you all in the channel so you know.
And we'll also update your Looker dashboard with the URL so we can immediately start tracking performance.
Awesome.
Will you just ping me once those new guides are live, just ping me so that I can QA and publish type thing?
You mean the other three?
Yeah, the next three.
I can QA the blog update right after this and push that live.
But I just want to make sure we have a good workflow for the additional articles coming that will actually live under the guide section.
Okay.
All right.
Yeah.
As soon as we stage them, I will tag you and then you can publish the first batch.
Then from then on, we can publish ourselves just to avoid giving you more work to do.
But I think for this first batch, it's totally cool for you to QA.
Okay.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
All right.
Great.
Thank you, everyone.
I'll see you in January.
Have a nice holiday and new year.
Bye-bye.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress, new strategy, and holiday planning.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=274.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Content Strategy:** Shift from foundational topics to deep-dive articles on specific pain points (e.g., efficiency), targeting commercial keywords and Cresta's core product clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=274.0)
  - [**Featured Image Direction:** The initial AI-generated images were rejected as off-brand. New creative will be developed using the Cresta homepage as the sole style guide.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=627.0)
  - [**Holiday Plan:** Weekly syncs are paused for two weeks (Dec 19 & 26) for async updates. Production continues with 5 articles next week, then pauses for one week.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=819.0)
  - [**Workflow Automation:** An email notification will be set up to alert Nicky when new drafts are ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)

## Topics

### New Content Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Shift from foundational, top-of-funnel content to more specific, commercial-intent articles that address distinct pain points.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=274.0)
  - [**Basis:** Over 40 new blog topic ideas were added to the calendar, derived from Devin's feedback and the product feature matrix.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=274.0)
  - [**Focus Clusters:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
      - [Contact Center Operations](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
      - [Agent Performance & Coaching](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
      - [Conversational Insights & Voice of Customer](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
  - [**New Drafts:** 5 drafts are ready for review, including commercial keywords like "best AI agent platforms."](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=414.0)
      - [**Note:** These articles require more feedback to correctly position Cresta against competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)

### Featured Image Direction

  - [**Initial Test:** Images generated with Nano Banana for the "How to Improve Contact Center Productivity" article were presented.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=527.0)
  - [**Feedback:** Russell rejected the style as "classic AI tech look" and not aligned with the new brand.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=627.0)
  - [**New Direction:** The Cresta homepage will be the sole style guide for new creative.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The homepage provides a clear, approved visual standard.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [**Goal:** Create abstract, branded images that could fit seamlessly on the homepage.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=734.0)
      - [**Action:** Katya (designer) will create new examples based on this direction.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)

### Content Workflow & Cadence

  - [**Publishing:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [**Calibration Refresh:** Annie will QA and publish this first piece today.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [**Next 3 Articles:** Annie will QA and publish this initial batch to establish the workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=973.0)
      - [**Future Articles:** GrowthX will handle self-publishing to reduce Cresta's workload.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=989.0)
  - [**Holiday Schedule:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=819.0)
      - [**Syncs:** Paused for two weeks (Dec 19 & 26); updates will be async.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=819.0)
      - [**Production:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
          - [**Next Week:** 5 articles delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
          - [**Week of Dec 29:** Production paused.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
          - [**Week of Jan 5:** Production resumes.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The 10-week sprint was designed to accommodate this holiday period.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Nicky:** Review the "variant alternatives" blog today.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=215.0)
  - [**Annie:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [QA and publish the "Calibration Refresh" piece today.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [QA and publish the next 3 articles once staged by GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=973.0)
  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)
      - [Set up the email automation for draft reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)
      - [Share the new featured image examples from Katya.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
      - [Tag Steve in Slack for feedback on the new creative direction.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=601.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Deliver 5 articles next week before the holiday pause.](https://fathom.video/share/gkkLW1c8Mxtqh_sgi3mHTT63J2RQHxnv?tab=summary&timestamp=880.0)

action_items.description: Send feedback on 'variant alternatives' blog to Aida
QA/publish blog calibration refresh; ping Aida to update Looker
Set up email automation to notify Nicky when drafts are ready
Post Nano Banana featured image examples in Slack; tag Steve
Brief Katya on homepage-led abstract featured image examples; share when ready
Send async updates to Nicky, Russell, Annie Dec 18–Jan 1
Stage 3 guides; tag Annie to QA/publish; then tag Nicky, Russell, Annie in Slack and update Looker
